title: Important Cultural Property (Japan)

infobox.value: (重要文化財, *jūyō bunkazai*)[[note 1]](#cite_note-1) is an item officially classified as [Tangible Cultural Property](/wiki/Tangible_Cultural_Property_(Japan) "Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)") by the [Japanese government](/wiki/Government_of_Japan "Government of Japan")'s [Agency for Cultural Affairs](/wiki/Agency_for_Cultural_Affairs "Agency for Cultural Affairs") ([Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology](/wiki/Ministry_of_Education,_Culture,_Sports,_Science_and_Technology "Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology")) and judged to be of particular importance to the history, arts, and culture of the [Japanese people](/wiki/Japanese_people "Japanese people").[[1]](#cite_note-ACA-2)
The term is often shortened into just *jūbun* (重文).
In this article, capitals indicate an official designation as opposed to a simple, unofficial definition, e.g "Cultural Properties" as opposed to "cultural properties".

[Yokohama Sankei Garden](http://www.sankeien.or.jp/history/index.html) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20170430214121/http://www.sankeien.or.jp/history/index.html) 2017-04-30 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), Sankei-en's official site accessed on November 3, 2009 (in Japanese)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Object of high historical or artistic value

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/Sankeien_Rinshunkaku_2.jpg/330px-Sankeien_Rinshunkaku_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sankeien_Rinshunkaku_2.jpg)

[Sankei-en](/wiki/Sankei-en "Sankei-en")'s Rinshunkaku in [Yokohama](/wiki/Yokohama "Yokohama") is a nationally designated Important Cultural Property of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan").

An **Important Cultural Property** (重要文化財, *jūyō bunkazai*)[[note 1]](#cite_note-1) is an item officially classified as [Tangible Cultural Property](/wiki/Tangible_Cultural_Property_(Japan) "Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)") by the [Japanese government](/wiki/Government_of_Japan "Government of Japan")'s [Agency for Cultural Affairs](/wiki/Agency_for_Cultural_Affairs "Agency for Cultural Affairs") ([Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology](/wiki/Ministry_of_Education,_Culture,_Sports,_Science_and_Technology "Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology")) and judged to be of particular importance to the history, arts, and culture of the [Japanese people](/wiki/Japanese_people "Japanese people").[[1]](#cite_note-ACA-2)

## Classification of Cultural Properties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Important_Cultural_Property_(Japan)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Classification of Cultural Properties")]

To protect the [cultural heritage](/wiki/Cultural_heritage "Cultural heritage") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties was created as a "designation system" (指定制度) under which important items are appropriated as Cultural Properties,[[note 2]](#cite_note-3) thus imposing restrictions to their alteration, repair and export.[[1]](#cite_note-ACA-2) Besides the "designation system", there exists a "registration system" (登録制度), which guarantees a lower level of protection and support to [Registered Cultural Properties](/wiki/Cultural_Properties_of_Japan "Cultural Properties of Japan").

Cultural Properties are classified according to their nature. Items designated as Tangible Cultural Properties (as opposed to [Intangible Cultural Properties](/wiki/Intangible_Cultural_Properties_of_Japan "Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan")), cultural products of high historical or artistic value such as structures, paintings, sculptures, handicrafts, calligraphic works, ancient books, historic documents, archeological artifacts and other such items, can later, if they satisfy certain criteria, be designated either Important Cultural Properties or [National Treasures](/wiki/National_Treasures_of_Japan "National Treasures of Japan") (国宝), for especially valuable items. The designation can take place at the city (市定重要文化財, *city designated Important Cultural Property*), prefectural (県定重要文化財, *prefecturally designated Important Cultural Property*) or national (国定重要文化財, *nationally designated Important Cultural Property*) level. In this last case the designating agency is often not specified. Varying levels of designation can coexist. For example, [Sankei-en](/wiki/Sankei-en "Sankei-en"), a traditional [Japanese-style garden](/wiki/Japanese_garden "Japanese garden") in [Naka Ward](/wiki/Naka-ku,_Yokohama "Naka-ku, Yokohama"), [Yokohama](/wiki/Yokohama "Yokohama"), is both city and nationally designated as an Important Cultural Properties.[[2]](#cite_note-officialsite-4)

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Important_Cultural_Property_(Japan)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Examples")]

* [![Seiganto-ji, Wakayama Prefecture](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/73/Seigantoji05s1920.jpg/250px-Seigantoji05s1920.jpg)](/wiki/File:Seigantoji05s1920.jpg "Seiganto-ji, Wakayama Prefecture")

  [Seiganto-ji](/wiki/Seiganto-ji "Seiganto-ji"), [Wakayama Prefecture](/wiki/Wakayama_Prefecture "Wakayama Prefecture")
* [![A Nio at Hōryū-ji, Nara](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Horyu-ji15s3200.jpg/250px-Horyu-ji15s3200.jpg)](/wiki/File:Horyu-ji15s3200.jpg "A Nio at Hōryū-ji, Nara")

  A [Nio](/wiki/Nio_(Buddhism) "Nio (Buddhism)") at [Hōryū-ji](/wiki/H%C5%8Dry%C5%AB-ji "Hōryū-ji"), [Nara](/wiki/Nara,_Nara "Nara, Nara")
* [![Kōchi Castle, Kōchi Prefecture](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/Kochi_Castle04s3872.jpg/250px-Kochi_Castle04s3872.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kochi_Castle04s3872.jpg "Kōchi Castle, Kōchi Prefecture")

  [Kōchi Castle](/wiki/K%C5%8Dchi_Castle "Kōchi Castle"), [Kōchi Prefecture](/wiki/K%C5%8Dchi_Prefecture "Kōchi Prefecture")
* [![Nihon Bridge in Nihonbashi, Tokyo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Nihonbashi_12.jpg/250px-Nihonbashi_12.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nihonbashi_12.jpg "Nihon Bridge in Nihonbashi, Tokyo")

  Nihon Bridge in [Nihonbashi](/wiki/Nihonbashi "Nihonbashi"), [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo")
* [![Tsūjun Bridge, Kumamoto Prefecture](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Tsujyunkyo.jpg/250px-Tsujyunkyo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tsujyunkyo.jpg "Tsūjun Bridge, Kumamoto Prefecture")

  [Tsūjun Bridge](/wiki/Ts%C5%ABjun_Bridge "Tsūjun Bridge"), [Kumamoto Prefecture](/wiki/Kumamoto_Prefecture "Kumamoto Prefecture")
* [![Holy Resurrection Cathedral in Tokyo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Nikolai-do.jpg/250px-Nikolai-do.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nikolai-do.jpg "Holy Resurrection Cathedral in Tokyo")

  [Holy Resurrection Cathedral](/wiki/Holy_Resurrection_Cathedral "Holy Resurrection Cathedral") in Tokyo
* [![The Former Hokkaidō government office building](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/65/Hokkaido_Prefectural_Office02s5s4272.jpg/250px-Hokkaido_Prefectural_Office02s5s4272.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hokkaido_Prefectural_Office02s5s4272.jpg "The Former Hokkaidō government office building")

  The [Former Hokkaidō government office building](/wiki/Former_Hokkaid%C5%8D_Government_Office "Former Hokkaidō Government Office")
* [![Tsurugaoka Hachimangū in Kamakura, Kanagawa prefecture](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9b/TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg/250px-TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg)](/wiki/File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg "Tsurugaoka Hachimangū in Kamakura, Kanagawa prefecture")

  [Tsurugaoka Hachimangū](/wiki/Tsurugaoka_Hachimang%C5%AB "Tsurugaoka Hachimangū") in [Kamakura, Kanagawa](/wiki/Kamakura,_Kanagawa "Kamakura, Kanagawa") prefecture
* [![Meiji Seimei Kan in Tokyo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/Meiji_Yasuda_Life_Insurance_Company_Head_Office_2009.jpg/250px-Meiji_Yasuda_Life_Insurance_Company_Head_Office_2009.jpg)](/wiki/File:Meiji_Yasuda_Life_Insurance_Company_Head_Office_2009.jpg "Meiji Seimei Kan in Tokyo")

  [Meiji Seimei Kan](/wiki/Meiji_Seimei_Kan "Meiji Seimei Kan") in Tokyo
* [![Hatsuhana tea caddy, one of the special tea utensils, kept at the Tokugawa Memorial Foundation](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/Hatsuhana_Katatsuki%2C_front_view_%28black_and_white%29.jpg/250px-Hatsuhana_Katatsuki%2C_front_view_%28black_and_white%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hatsuhana_Katatsuki,_front_view_(black_and_white).jpg "Hatsuhana tea caddy, one of the special tea utensils, kept at the Tokugawa Memorial Foundation")

  *Hatsuhana* tea caddy, one of the [special tea utensils](/wiki/Special_tea_utensils "Special tea utensils"), kept at the Tokugawa Memorial Foundation
